title: Pauletia tenella
intro: Pauletia tenella, also known as Pauletia, is a plant species of the genus Pauletia in the family Asteraceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported health benefits, including antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pauletia tenella include sesquiterpenes, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Pauletia tenella is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Pauletia tenella is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to tropical Africa.
